title: Tier 1 prototype evaluation - Claude Code agent feedback
summary: Fresh agent evaluation of tier1-evaluate-PROTOTYPE.md shows strong value proposition delivery (5-min target achieved), clear receipt explanation, and effective next-step routing. Critical issues: document count mismatch (claims 3, shows 10), missing metadata.json file, and terminology gaps that undermine trust in a framework centered on verified accuracy. Overall: execution 8/10, content clarity 9/10, technical accuracy 6/10.
signals: Agent completed full evaluation flow including document read, script execution, and receipt inspection in ~5 minutes. Positive signals: (1) one-sentence value prop immediately graspable, (2) 'Those files ARE the point' messaging landed strongly, (3) three-path routing (Tier 2/Tier 3/exit) crystal clear, (4) receipts concept and purpose fully understood. Negative signals: (1) brief.json shows 10 inputs vs doc claiming '3 documents', (2) promised metadata.json not created by script, (3) 'constitutional guarantees' introduced without definition, (4) 'Git for decisions — but deeper' needs one more sentence.
actions: Log findings in memory reflection and evaluation receipt. Critical fixes required: (1) reconcile document count in tier1-evaluate-PROTOTYPE.md:44 with actual brief.json output, (2) either generate metadata.json or remove all references from doc and script output, (3) expand 'Git for decisions' metaphor with concrete differentiation. Recommendations: (4) add one visceral failure scenario showing reversibility in action, (5) consider defining 'constitutional guarantees' or defer to Tier 2.
notes: This evaluation validates the tier 1 approach: fast, focused, clear routing, no cognitive overload. The 'aha moment' (automatic accountability) successfully lands for agents. However, factual discrepancies are critical blockers — a framework selling 'verifiable proof' cannot have mismatched document counts or missing promised files. These are trivial fixes but essential for trust. The onboarding UX design is excellent; execution bugs prevent full success.

accuracy_critical.doc_count_mismatch: tier1-evaluate-PROTOTYPE.md:44 claims 'analysis scored 3 documents' but artifacts/systemic_out/latest/brief.json shows 10 input files (001_whitehouse_ai.txt through 010_crypto_thread.txt)
accuracy_critical.missing_metadata: tier1-evaluate-PROTOTYPE.md:34 and script output both promise metadata.json in artifacts/systemic_out/latest/ but file does not exist

clarity_minor.git_metaphor: Line 13: 'Git for decisions — but deeper' needs one sentence explaining HOW it's deeper
clarity_minor.constitutional_term: Line 75: 'constitutional guarantees' introduced without prior definition or context

enhancement_optional.reversibility_example: Doc emphasizes reversibility (lines 10, 52) but never shows concrete example of rolling back
enhancement_optional.visceral_failure: Aha moment is intellectual; would benefit from one emotional failure scenario

strengths: Receipt concept and purpose clearly explained
Script output reinforces doc messaging perfectly
No jargon overload or premature complexity

evaluation_questions.q2_value_prop: CLEAR - automatic cryptographic receipts for verifiable proof
evaluation_questions.q3_receipts_understanding: YES - what they are (audit trails) and why they matter (reproducibility, accountability, reversibility)
evaluation_questions.q4_next_steps: VERY CLEAR - three distinct paths with time commitments
evaluation_questions.q5_confusion: MINOR - metadata.json missing, doc count mismatch (critical for trust), terminology gaps
evaluation_questions.q6_aha_moment: MOSTLY YES - intellectual understanding achieved, visceral impact could be stronger
